prompt:
Add two cache-economics modules to @prune/agent-sdk-adapter, exported from the package index:

1. Session-idle cache guard — `src/idle-guard.ts` exporting `evaluateIdleGuard`, `heartbeatThreshold(ttl)`, `TTL_WINDOW_MS`, and an `IdleGuardInput` type with fields { ttl: "5m" | "1h", model, cacheablePrefixTokens, idleMs, continuationProbability, maxHeartbeats, heartbeatsSoFar }. It decides whether to send a keep-alive heartbeat before the prompt-cache TTL window expires, or let the cache lapse. A heartbeat costs a cache READ of the prefix; letting it lapse costs a cache WRITE on the next real turn — so the break-even continuation probability is READ/(WRITE − READ) per Anthropic's cache pricing multipliers (read 0.1×, write 1.25× for 5m TTL, 2× for 1h), which `heartbeatThreshold` returns. Respect maxHeartbeats; an idle time already past the window means the cache is gone (nothing to guard).

2. Delta cache-resend planner — `src/delta-resend.ts` exporting `analyzeDeltaResend` and a `PrefixBlock` type. Given the previous and next turn's prefix block sequences, find the surviving leading run and diagnose what a mid-prefix change does: identical prefixes → no divergence; appended blocks are a normal diverged tail (not a poison); next being a strict prefix of previous is not a rewrite; an in-place change at the top of the prompt strands everything after it (poison) unless nothing of cacheable size is stranded; a rejoined run below the provider's minimum cacheable size cannot realize savings. Report exact economics: full-bust re-write cost vs delta-resend cost and the saving, using the model's real input/cache rates and the 5m vs 1h write multipliers; below-minimum surviving runs report zero realizable saving.
notes: Reference: agent-sdk-adapter N5+N2 2026-06-03. Two new modules (+476 impl lines) against test specs.
